top_signals.title: Gilts Are Giving Burnham a Pass for Now, Says Aegon’s Lynch
top_signals.summary: Gilt markets have shrugged off the lack of economic policy clarity from Andy Burnham, the likely successor to replace Keir Starmer as UK prime minister, according to Aegon Asset Management’s James Lynch.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: The World’s Second-Biggest Company Isn’t a ‘Rock Star’ Anymore
top_signals.summary: Google had a standout year, but now, a shift in the AI trade has it lagging the Nasdaq 100.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Uniqlo’s plan to dominate global fashion
top_signals.summary: The clothing retailer forged in Japan’s post-bubble economy is pinning its hopes on Europe and North America

top_signals.title: Brewdog chief executive quits after only one year
top_signals.summary: The boss of Brewdog has stepped down barely more than a year after taking up the role in the latest twist in the long-running saga over the management of the beleaguered beer business.
